title: Hali Ke

{{Quote|This is the true art of genetic selection and manipulation. A human is naturally a learning creature, but it is also violent, selfish, lustful, and undisciplined. So we must walk the knife-edge between suppressing the factors that lead to disobedience and destroying that prized capacity for applying intelligence and aggression.|Hali Ke|<ref name="RC:HC" />}}
'''Hali Ke''' was a [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] and the senior research [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticist]] of the [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] facilities on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], they spent many [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] with [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], interviewing and researching the traits that made [[Gender/Legends|him]] the ideal [[Clone template/Legends|template]] for cloning. Ke's research on Fett and other [[Human/Legends|Human]] subjects led to the Kaminoan penning a memorandum regarding findings from the research, as well as their observations on Humans as a [[Species/Legends|species]].

The Kaminoan concluded the memorandum by stating the sterility was in fact not achievable for an [[Army/Legends|army]] of humans, given the species' physiological needs. However, Ke outlined multiple strategies for minimizing any negative effects the ability to [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduce]] might have on the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], which primarily consisted of training regimens and limiting contact with non-[[Clone trooper/Legends|clones]].

==Biography==
===Understanding Humans===
{{Quote|In attempting to understand the mental processes and constructs that have sprung from their tangled genetic code, I have interviewed and psychoanalyzed humans from both ends of this broad spectrum. I've spoken — at a safe remove — with a pirate from the Fair Hollis system who lacked the ability to see other organic beings as anything other than objects, and with Kardavan penitents who had spent their entire lives attempting to ameliorate the suffering of beings with whom they shared no bond of kinship or community. Any attempt to summarize the habits, attitudes and psyches of humans would either be so lengthy as to defy useful summary, or so general as to be useless.|Hali Ke|<ref name="SW:EGWAC">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-7-the-grand-army-of-the-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 7 — The Grand Army of the Republic}}</ref>}}
In [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] Hali Ke was the senior research [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticist]] of the [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] facilities on<ref name="SW:EGWAC" /> the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], Ke penned an internal memorandum that outlined the results of the Kaminoan's research<ref name="SW:EGWAC" /> on [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]<ref name="RC:HC" /> [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] and of cloning [[Human/Legends|Human]] subjects. This detailed their extensive research on the society, mentality, and physiology of Humans, which included interviews on a diverse range of subjects, including those performed with a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] hailing from the [[Fair Hollis system/Legends|Fair Hollis system]] as well as [[Kardavan penitents]] devoted to helping other beings. Contrasting the wide range of mental constructs Humans portrayed, Ke noted that the members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] were capable of an extraordinarily wide range of ethical and moral diversity, with notable extremes on both ends of the spectrum. The Kaminoan concluded that Humans dominated [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] due to their extreme adaptability to become compatible with the wide range of environments they were found in.<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />

[[File:Kamino_Facility.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Ke extensively studied the clones of Jango Fett created on Kamino.]]
During Ke's research, the Kaminoan logged many interview sessions with [[Clone template/Legends|prime clone]] Jango Fett, who they felt embodied the contradictions Humans portrayed. They noted Fett's competency in his chosen profession, but when they suggested that Fett lacked morality in a field primarily devoted to [[Death/Legends|killing]], the [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] vehemently disagreed. The Kaminoan observed Fett's distain for attachment,<ref name="SW:EGWAC" /> while at the same time having the ability to call upon a hundred [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], Mandalorian and otherwise,<ref name="RC:HC" /> to assist in the training of what would become the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]]. Although Ke noted that Fett cared little for the considerable sum of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] he received for his biological assistance in creating the clone [[Army/Legends|army]], the Kaminoan wrote that the hunter would gently play with his adopted son, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba]], after returning from a violent hunt.<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />

Continuing the memorandum, they cited differences between Fett and his clones that would be introduced. These qualities mentioned by the geneticist included higher [[Lung/Legends|lung]] capacity, increased fast-twitch musculature, improved stamina and recovery times, and minor allergic reactions and astigmatism. The clones were also produced to be more docile and inclined towards team identity, although some of Fett's independence was still expected to unavoidably surface, according to Ke.<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />

===Clone sterility===
{{Quote|It is certainly irregular to recommend that we deliver an army of clone units able to reproduce. But the TRE and Puralan experiments, as well as my experience with humans, tell me that we have no choice if we also want an army that can fight effectively… By following this program, I am confident that incidents of clones reproducing will remain minimal, and their impact further minimized through contrafactual public communications by the customer.|Hali Ke|<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />}}
Ke's research continued to the issue of engineered sterility in the clone army. Citing results from other Human clones engineered by Kamino, including projects for [[Tarshan Ring Excavations]] and the [[Lords of Purala IV]], the Kaminoan geneticist concluded that the clones' ability to [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduce]] was in fact vital to their performance. Ke projected the unusual lack of sterility in the clones would be mitigated by channeling basic Human impulses towards unit cohesion and minimizing the army's contact with non-clones. The Kaminoan also suggested that bioengineered contingency planning should be implemented for behavioral modification, which they noted could be useful if further modification was requested by their employers. Ke concluded by stating that research would persist as Kaminoan geneticists continued to work on a species they considered to be both fascinating and confounding.<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|It is truly a pleasure to be producing humans again – and this time with a budget that allows us to linger in the twists and turns of that fascinating genome. Of all the species I have examined and augmented, humans may be my favorite.|Hali Ke|<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />}}
Hali Ke rose to the rank of senior research geneticist on Kamino and was fascinated by the vast range of physiological and biological contrasts that the Human species portrayed. The geneticist considered the species to be among their favorite to examine, and interviewed and psychoanalyzed a broad spectrum of Humans from vastly different backgrounds, the most notable being Jango Fett.<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I was intrigued by that passage and wondered what the context might be. In arriving at an answer, I also tried to address something else that had intrigued me: Why would anyone create an army of clones that could reproduce? This was one of my favorite pieces written for Warfare; cutting it really hurt and I'm very pleased to be able to share it at long last.|Jason Fry|<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />}}
Hali Ke was first mentioned in the [[2004]] novel [[Republic Commando: Hard Contact|''Hard Contact'']], the first novel in the ''[[Republic Commando]]'' series written by [[Karen Traviss]], which featured an excerpt from the Kaminoan's internal memorandum.<ref name="RC:HC" /> The passage was later expanded to include the entire memorandum, which was written—with the exception of Traviss' portion—by [[Jason Fry]] and was intended to be featured in the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''. Although the memorandum did not appear in the book, it was finally released in the [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|Author's Cut]] of the book on [[StarWars.com]] in [[2014]].<ref name="SW:EGWAC" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]'' {{1stm}}
